---
title: "Readings"
subtitle: "Three short reads – the shared language and core ideas behind the day."
listing:
  contents: "readings/*.qmd"
  type: default
  sort: "order"
  fields: [title, description, reading-time]
  date-format: ""
---

You don't need any technical background for this masterclass, and you don't need to read these beforehand – read them whenever you like: to warm up before we start, to go deeper on a concept during the day, or to revisit afterwards. They walk from *what AI is*, to *when to trust it*, to *why delivering an AI project is different*.

:::: {.callout-tip}
## AI is more than text
This course focuses on generative AI (the text, image and code models reshaping everyday work), but AI is a much bigger field – machine learning, computer vision, audio, robotics. Want to feel how non-text AI works? Try [**Google's Teachable Machine**](https://teachablemachine.withgoogle.com) – train your own image or audio classifier in two minutes, right in the browser. No code, no account. It's the clearest way to see that "pattern-matching" isn't just about words.
::::

:::: {#listing}
::::

:::: {.callout-note}
## Want to go further?
Everything here is drawn from the free companion book, [*Conversation, Not Delegation*](https://michael-borck.github.io/conversation-not-delegation/). It's optional, but if a topic grabs you, that's where to dig.
::::
